quickFacts: Port wine tastings in Vila Nova de Gaia start from €5
The São Bento station azulejo tiles are free to admire
Francesinha is Porto's meat-lover sandwich — try before judging
Book Livraria Lello tickets online (€5, redeemable on books)
foodScene: Francesinha is a gut-busting tower of meats, cheese, and spicy sauce — and it's glorious. Pastéis de nata (custard tarts) everywhere. Bacalhau (salt cod) done 1,000 ways. Wash it all down with a €1 espresso or a glass of Vinho Verde.

places.name: Ribeira District
places.caption: UNESCO waterfront
places.description: Narrow colourful houses tumbling down to the Douro River. Grab a Super Bock at a riverside café and watch the rabelo boats float by.

places.name: Livraria Lello
places.caption: World's most beautiful bookshop
places.description: Neo-Gothic staircase that allegedly inspired Hogwarts. Go at opening to beat the crowds. Your €5 entry becomes a book voucher.

places.name: Dom Luís I Bridge
places.caption: Double-deck iron icon
places.description: Walk the upper deck for breathtaking views of both Porto and Gaia. Designed by a student of Gustave Eiffel.

places.name: São Bento Station
places.caption: Azulejo masterpiece
places.description: A working train station covered in 20,000 hand-painted blue tiles depicting Portuguese history. It's free — just walk in and look up.

places.name: Clérigos Tower
places.caption: Baroque bell tower
places.description: Climb 240 steps for the best 360° panorama of Porto. The church below is free and features stunning gilded woodwork.

places.name: Vila Nova de Gaia
places.caption: Port wine cellars
places.description: Cross the bridge to the south bank for port tastings at historic lodges like Taylor's, Graham's, or Sandeman. Cable car back up for river views.

places.name: Jardins do Palácio de Cristal
places.caption: Hilltop gardens
places.description: Peacocks roam freely in these manicured gardens with sweeping views over the Douro and Atlantic. Perfect for a lazy afternoon.

places.name: Foz do Douro
places.caption: Beachside neighbourhood
places.description: Where the Douro meets the Atlantic. Walk the coastal promenade, watch surfers, and grab fresh seafood at Praia da Luz.

places.name: Bolhão Market
places.caption: Freshly renovated food hall
places.description: Two floors of fresh produce, cured meats, cheese, and flowers. The vendors are characters. Don't miss the bifana (pork sandwich) stalls.

places.name: Day Trip: Douro Valley
places.caption: Vineyard-lined river
places.description: UNESCO-listed wine region just 2 hours by train. Terraced vineyards, wine estates, and boat cruises through dramatic gorges.

places.name: Port Wine Cellars (Vila Nova de Gaia)
places.caption: Wine tasting
places.description: Cross the bridge to Gaia for tastings at Taylor's, Graham's, Sandeman and dozens more. Cellar tours explain the ageing process.

places.name: Mercado do Bolhão
places.caption: Historic market
places.description: Recently restored Art Nouveau market. Fresh produce, bacalhau, cheese, flowers and a food court. Porto's food soul.

places.name: Douro Valley Day Trip
places.caption: Wine country cruise
places.description: UNESCO-listed terraced vineyards. River cruise, wine tastings and lunch at a quinta. Train or boat — both scenic.

places.name: Church of São Francisco
places.caption: Golden interior
places.description: Gothic church with an interior covered in 300 kg of gold leaf. The catacombs underneath house an ossuary.
